misconceptions.title: Graphing Incorrect Boundary Lines
misconceptions.description: Incorrectly using a solid line for strict inequalities shows a mistaken belief that points on the boundary are included in the solution set. This error often stems from not fully understanding the difference between strict and inclusive inequalities.
successIndicators: Use dashed line for strict inequalities

misconceptions.title: Switching X and Y in Equations
misconceptions.description: Switching x and y indicates confusion about the role of variables in an equation, leading to incorrect solutions. Students might believe repositioning has no significant effect on the solution.
successIndicators: Identify roles of x and y correctly

misconceptions.title: Clearing Fractions Incorrectly
misconceptions.description: Attempting to clear fractions by incorrectly multiplying terms is a procedural error that results from misunderstanding fraction operations and equation balance.
successIndicators: Multiply all terms by appropriate common factor

misconceptions.title: Using Incorrect Inverse Operations
misconceptions.description: Using addition instead of subtraction as the inverse operation suggests a procedural confusion about maintaining equation equality, especially when solving for variables.
